executiveSummary.summary: The market need is real and the timing is good, but you're entering a crowded space where established ATS players have distribution advantages and customer trust. The $500/month price point may be too high for standalone screening without full ATS integration. Your biggest challenge isn't technical—it's proving AI accuracy and building trust with risk-averse HR teams.

marketSize.tam: $3.8B global recruiting software market (Grand View Research 2023)
marketSize.sam: $800M mid-market ATS/screening segment in North America
marketSize.methodology: Based on ~50,000 US companies with 50-500 employees, assuming 2-4% early adopter penetration

competitors.name: HireVue
competitors.description: AI-powered video interviewing and assessment platform
competitors.strength: Established brand, proven AI bias mitigation

competitors.name: Pymetrics
competitors.weakness: Limited to assessment, not resume screening

competitors.name: Greenhouse
competitors.description: Leading ATS with basic AI scoring features
competitors.weakness: AI features are basic, expensive for mid-market

competitors.name: Lever
competitors.description: Modern ATS with candidate relationship management

competitors.name: Workable
competitors.description: Mid-market focused ATS with AI sourcing

marketAnalysis.positioning: AI-first resume screening for companies who can't afford enterprise ATS but need better than manual review
marketAnalysis.timing: Good - AI adoption accelerating, but bias concerns are growing

valueProposition.coreProblem: HR teams at 50-500 person companies spend 10-15 hours per role manually screening resumes, missing good candidates and introducing unconscious bias
valueProposition.targetCustomer: HR managers/directors at growing companies who receive 100+ applications per role but lack enterprise ATS budgets
valueProposition.uniqueAngle: Transparent AI scoring with explainable decisions, designed for mid-market budgets and workflows
valueProposition.whyNow: Remote work explosion increased application volumes 3-5x while HR teams stayed same size, plus AI accuracy crossed usability threshold

businessModel.revenueStrategy: Monthly SaaS subscription with usage-based overage fees
businessModel.pricingFramework: $299/month base (up to 500 applications) + $0.50 per additional application. Lower than your proposed $500 to improve adoption.
businessModel.scalabilityAssessment: Good - marginal costs are low, but customer success becomes critical at scale to prevent churn

technicalFeasibility.recommendedStack: Python/Django backend, React frontend, OpenAI/Anthropic APIs, PostgreSQL, AWS/Railway hosting

buildVsBuy.rationale: Use existing APIs like Affinda or Sovren to avoid PDF complexity
Core differentiator, need custom prompts and logic
Use Auth0 or similar to focus on core features

risks.description: Greenhouse, Lever, or Workable adds sophisticated AI screening within 12 months
risks.mitigation: Focus on transparency and explainability as differentiator, build integration partnerships

risks.description: HR teams don't trust AI for hiring decisions due to bias concerns and legal risks
risks.mitigation: Emphasize human-in-the-loop workflow, provide bias detection features, get legal review of claims

risks.description: Customer acquisition costs exceed budget before achieving product-market fit
risks.mitigation: Start with warm network, focus on content marketing, consider freemium model

risks.description: EEOC or state regulations restrict AI use in hiring
risks.mitigation: Stay current on regulations, build audit trails, consult employment lawyer

risks.description: AI accuracy insufficient for production use, high false positive/negative rates
risks.mitigation: Extensive testing with real job/resume pairs, human feedback loops, confidence scoring

week1to4: Interview 15 HR managers at target companies about current screening process
Research legal requirements for AI hiring tools
Set up development environment and basic architecture
week5to8: Develop AI scoring algorithm with 3-5 job types
Create simple web interface for testing
week9to12: Build full MVP with user management
Prepare go-to-market materials and pricing page

decisionAgenda.question: Should you build standalone tool or integrate with existing ATS platforms?

options.tradeoff: Higher margins but harder customer acquisition vs easier adoption
Faster adoption but lower margins and dependency on partners

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start standalone but design API-first architecture for future integrations. Test standalone model first to prove value.

decisionAgenda.question: What's your customer acquisition strategy with limited budget?

options.tradeoff: Direct but expensive and time-consuming for solo founder
Cheaper but slower, requires consistent content creation
Leverage their networks but share revenue

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start with content marketing while building MVP, then add targeted outbound to warm leads from content engagement.

decisionAgenda.question: How do you handle AI bias and transparency concerns?

options.option: Black box AI with high accuracy focus

options.tradeoff: Lower accuracy but builds trust and reduces legal risk

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Choose explainable AI approach. Provide scoring rationale and bias detection features as key differentiators.

decisionAgenda.question: Should you raise funding or bootstrap?

options.tradeoff: Maintain control but limited runway and growth speed
More resources but dilution and investor pressure

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Bootstrap for 6 months to prove initial traction, then consider raising if growth demands it.
